Line height

Default leading paragraph. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog so this paragraph wraps across several lines at normal spacing.

Double-spaced paragraph (line-height: 2). The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog so this paragraph wraps across several lines with clearly wider gaps between them.

Percentage paragraph (line-height: 160%). The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog so this paragraph wraps with moderately wider spacing.

Fixed-length paragraph (line-height: 28pt). The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog so successive baselines sit exactly 28 points apart.

Tight paragraph (line-height: 1.05) inside a border. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Lines sit closer than the default without ascenders escaping the border above.

default cell leading with text long enough to wrap onto multiple lines inside this table cell for comparison roomy cell (line-height: 200%) with text long enough to wrap onto multiple lines inside this table cell
